ddls:
CREATE TABLE "exams" (
  "exam_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exam */,
  "exam_name" TEXT /* Name of the exam (e.g., p1, p2) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the exam (YYYY-MM-DD format) */,
  "day" TEXT /* Day of the week the exam is scheduled (e.g., mon, tue) */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the exam (e.g., 9.30-10.30) */,
  "subject_id" TEXT /* Subject ID for the exam (e.g., 15cse301) */,
  "semester" TEXT /* Semester in which the exam is conducted (e.g., II, IV) */,
  "number_of_students" INTEGER /* Number of students taking the exam */,
  "software_requirements" TEXT /* Software requirements for the exam (if any) */,
  "room_id" INTEGER /* Room ID where the exam is held */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Instructor ID responsible for the exam */,
  "exam_type" TEXT /* Type of the exam (e.g., midterm, final, quiz) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the exam (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the exam was scheduled */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the exam details */,
  "attendance_count" INTEGER /* Number of students who actually attended the exam */,
  "conflict_flag" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if there is a scheduling conflict */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exam_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exams_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exams_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exams_room_id FOREIGN KEY ("room_id") REFERENCES rooms ("room_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each subject (e.g., 15cse301) */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject (e.g., Data Structures and Algorithms) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department offering the subject (e.g., CSE, ECE) */,
  "semester" TEXT /* Semester in which the subject is offered (e.g., II, IV) */,
  "credit_hours" INTEGER /* Number of credit hours for the subject */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* Prerequisites for the subject (if any) */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Instructor ID responsible for the subject */,
  "syllabus_url" TEXT /* URL to the syllabus for the subject */,
  "max_students" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students that can enroll in the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rooms" (
  "room_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each room */,
  "room_name" TEXT /* Name of the room */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students the room can accommodate */,
  "location" TEXT /* Building and floor where the room is located */,
  "equipment" TEXT /* List of equipment available in the room */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("room_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instructors" (
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instructor */,
  "instructor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the instructor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the instructor */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the instructor belongs */,
  "office_location" TEXT /* Office location of the instructor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "major" TEXT /* Major of the student */,
  "year_of_study" INTEGER /* Year of study (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exam_attendance" (
  "attendance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attendance record */,
  "exam_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the exams table */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the students table */,
  "attendance_status" TEXT /* Status of the student's attendance (e.g., present, absent) */,
  "attendance_time" TEXT /* Time when the student's attendance was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exam_attendance_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exam_attendance_exam_id FOREIGN KEY ("exam_id") REFERENCES exams ("exam_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exam_conflicts" (
  "conflict_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conflict */,
  "exam_id1" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the exams table for the first conflicting exam */,
  "exam_id2" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the exams table for the second conflicting exam */,
  "conflict_type" TEXT /* Type of conflict (e.g., time, room) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the conflict has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conflict_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exam_conflicts_exam_id1 FOREIGN KEY ("exam_id1") REFERENCES exams ("exam_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exam_conflicts_exam_id2 FOREIGN KEY ("exam_id2") REFERENCES exams ("exam_id")
)
